upcomingEvents.title: Upcoming Events
upcomingEvents.body: The Vegas calendar is in reload mode: 10 future events remain, 2 sits inside the next 14 days, and 5 fall inside the next 30 days. The next named demand pulse is America250 Summer Fireworks on 2026-06-13 at Las Vegas Strip.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Translate the handoff between a just-finished event spike and the next named Vegas demand window into the right outreach and service cadence.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Once a major event cluster clears, Kevin gains more by exploiting the reload gap correctly than by continuing surge-mode behavior after the traffic wave has already passed.
quantResearchProtocol: Count named events inside the next 14-day and 30-day windows and compare them with the full active future-event book.
Separate live surge coverage from post-event cleanup and next-window reload work.
Name the current handoff event that just cleared and the next event that can rebuild account urgency.
Turn the calendar math into immediate outreach sequencing and service-readiness priorities.
inferenceConstraints: Do not describe the calendar as compressed if the main spike has already cleared.
Do not skip count math when recommending urgency or patience.
Do not detach event timing from the actual outreach order Kevin should run today.
outputRequirements: State whether the event book is in surge, cleanup, or reload mode.
Name the just-cleared anchor window and the next named demand pulse.
Give Kevin a concrete sequence for follow-up versus next-window prospecting.

sourceRecords.recordHint: 11 future event rows, 1 inside 14d, 3 inside 30d, next event America250 Summer Fireworks

notes: Calendar state moved from fully empty near-term window to a visible June reload window.
Event urgency is now count-backed and anchored to the June 6 fireworks handoff.

aiSalesStrategy.title: AI Sales Strategy
aiSalesStrategy.body: Across 151 Glide accounts, 151 are active service customers and 124 have a computed weekly oil estimate (fryer capacity x service cadence), totaling about 132,369 lbs/week. Kevin should lead with the highest-volume customers nearest the next event window, then work prospects and incomplete-telemetry accounts.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Turn verified Glide oil-usage estimates and the next event window into a concrete outreach order, with no synthetic scoring.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Kevin wins by prioritizing the customers whose fryer capacity and service cadence imply the most weekly oil, weighted by proximity to the next demand event, instead of an invented priority score.
quantResearchProtocol: Rank service customers by estimated weekly oil usage (fryer capacity x service-cadence changes per week).
Weight that ranking by days until the next named event window.
Separate prospects (no service schedule) and incomplete-telemetry customers from the rankable volume list.
Express the outreach plan as an ordered cadence Kevin can run immediately.
inferenceConstraints: Do not invent an account priority score; use only estimated oil usage and event adjacency.
Do not rank accounts that lack fryer capacity or a service schedule; surface them as incomplete telemetry.
Do not let market narrative replace account volume and event timing in the sequencing logic.
outputRequirements: State the customer count, the count with a computed oil estimate, and the total estimated weekly oil.
Provide the concrete outreach order Kevin should execute next.

sourceRecords.recordHint: Glide-synced restaurant rows with service schedule, oil type/form, contact, and derived estimated_oil_lbs_per_week
Glide-synced fryer capacity rows feeding the oil-usage estimate
Next demand-anchor event window for adjacency weighting

notes: Sales strategy is driven by verified Glide oil-usage estimates and event adjacency, not synthetic scores.
Accounts without fryer capacity or a service schedule are surfaced as incomplete telemetry.

restaurantAccounts.title: Restaurant Accounts
restaurantAccounts.body: 151 restaurant rows are synced from Glide; 151 carry a service schedule and 124 have enough fryer telemetry to estimate weekly oil usage. Accounts without a computed volume are surfaced honestly as incomplete telemetry rather than ranked on a synthetic score.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Separate total Glide account coverage from the subset that has enough telemetry to estimate weekly oil usage, so the field team works real volume instead of an invented ladder.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: The edge is in the accounts with both a service schedule and fryer capacity, since those produce a defensible weekly oil estimate; everything else is a coverage or data-completeness task.
quantResearchProtocol: Count total Glide restaurant rows, the subset with a service schedule (customers), and the subset with a computed oil estimate.
Compute the telemetry-completeness rate (oil-estimate rows over customer rows).
Separate prospects and incomplete-telemetry customers from the rankable volume list.
Translate the gap into the next field-execution focus.
inferenceConstraints: Do not rank accounts on a synthetic score.
Do not treat total coverage as conversion-ready volume.
Do not describe an account as actionable without a service schedule and fryer capacity.
outputRequirements: State total, customer, and oil-estimate counts explicitly.
Explain the next operational focus for Kevin and the field team.

sourceRecords.recordHint: Glide-synced restaurant rows with service schedule, oil type/form, contact, and derived estimated_oil_lbs_per_week
Glide-synced fryer capacity rows feeding the oil-usage estimate

notes: Account coverage and telemetry completeness are explicitly separated.
Ranking uses estimated oil usage, not a synthetic score layer.

fryerTracking.title: Fryer Equipment Tracking
fryerTracking.body: Fryer telemetry is materially populated now: 150 fryer rows exist and 150 carry positive capacity data. That is enough to support service-load planning, but direct fryer-count telemetry is still incomplete for precision scheduling.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Separate account-coverage completeness from equipment-telemetry completeness so service teams do not overstate readiness.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: A fully covered account roster with zero populated fryer counts creates false completeness unless field teams explicitly treat telemetry precision as missing.
quantResearchProtocol: Count fryer rows and populated fryer_count values separately.
Measure telemetry completeness as a percentage of covered sites.
Cross-reference the telemetry gap with near-term event pressure.
Translate the gap into a service-risk statement for field execution.
inferenceConstraints: Do not call the fryer dataset complete when fryer_count telemetry is still empty.
Do not ignore event timing while evaluating the telemetry gap.
Do not provide service guidance without stating the precision limit.
outputRequirements: State coverage and telemetry completeness separately.
Provide the posture until telemetry is complete.

sourceRecords.recordHint: 150 active fryer rows with 150 populated capacity rows
next named demand pulse is America250 Summer Fireworks on 2026-06-06

notes: Fryer telemetry is now materially populated instead of empty.
Current planning risk sits in sequencing and score recency, not equipment invisibility.
